what_it_does: A posted noticeboard note in the dragnet-development channel that records Tim's brief supportive comment on a proposal to turn dragnet cards into typed observations attached to file-nodes.
observations: Author session is 'tim' and channel is 'dragnet-development'
Body is a [point] response to a specific quoted proposal
Endorses the proposal with 'Cool. This generally sounds right on face value.'
Mentions using existing 'mark machinery' and file content-signatures for freshness
standouts: Endorsement of the proposal to model dragnet cards as observations on file-nodes
Use of existing mark machinery and recorded content-signatures to obtain freshness
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, state, title, author, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Markdown body with a quoted reference and a short reaction
concerns: Provides only a brief agreement without elaborated rationale or next steps
No thread or follow-up context is captured
notes: Tim's short note endorsing the idea of turning dragnet cards into file-node observations using existing mark machinery.
questions: What exactly is the 'mark machinery' being referenced?
What is the current structure of a dragnet card?
What does an 'observation' type require beyond being hung on a file-node address?

What is the scope and migration path for this change?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is intended as a brief comment/point on a linked proposal, and it does exactly that.
intention_signals: To signal agreement with a design direction and capture the rationale that dragnet cards should become typed observations on file-nodes, leveraging existing mark machinery and content-signature-based freshness.
novelty: The idea of deriving observation freshness from a file's recorded content-signature and treating dragnet cards as observations anchored to file-node addresses rather than standalone records.
contribution: A concise endorsement of the file-node observation model, plus the rationale that existing mark machinery and content-signatures can provide freshness, linked to the originating proposal item.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development commenting on a proposal to make dragnet cards typed observations attached to file-nodes, using existing mark machinery and file content-signatures for freshness.
intention_for_embedding: To record agreement with and a concise rationale for modeling dragnet cards as observations on file-nodes.
